author: chfast
content: I don't think this is impossible, but at least some people think it is too late to do so in Glamsterdam. I read this as it would be too big risk for already complicated hard fork.

author: draganrakita
content:
Would be good to hear from people that think it is too late for it. 

EIP-8037 have removed Selfdestruct state refund (as it is complex to do correctly), and EIP-7708 would remove burn logs if selfdestruct gets deprecated, so removal is kinda simplifying code even more.

author: spencer_tb
content:
My recent understanding was that it would be nice to add this ephemeral (transient) bytecode feature (via a new EIP) as a replacement for the SD use case alongside the removal. If that's the case shifting to Hegota feels reasonable. If people don't care about this feature then lets yeet it.

I did a quick SD removal in EELS and filled all the unique tests we have enabled in `bal-devnet-7`:
```
630 failed, 20,762 passed, 583 skipped <- around 3% failing
```
It doesn't feel that bad to remove from a testing perspective.

author: arnetheduck
content:
<@360491619402776577> I was looking into something like https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/793/ as well - one thing that I think would make sense would be to reuse the base structure of the beacon api (https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/) - this includes several things:
* primitives and their json/ssz encoding - ie in general, where possible, reuse the types from https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/blob/master/types/primitive.yaml so that we don't end up with pointless minor differences in how for example a number is string-encoded (`0x0 vs 0x` and the like)
* execution payloads and other spec types - the "shape" of objects in the beacon api _generally_ follows the shape of things as they travel on the gossip network and their SSZ encoding - by reusing these types, we would reduce the maintenance overhead of having to pointlessly reorder and rename the exact same fields from the beacon api just to send the same info to the execution api in a slightly different shape
* use of the canonical ssz/json encodings specified here: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/ssz/simple-serialize.md#json-mapping - this aids debugging and removes the need to double-specify things
* explicit encoding of fork in the http  headers -> we can then upgrade to an new hard fork "automatically" without having to come up with V2, V3 etc
